info.name: Stage Planner Sync API
info.description:
Test collection for POST /api/v1/stage_planner/sync

Covers all sync scenarios: new plans, updates, conflicts, deletions, and empty sync.

variable.description: Set after creating a plan via sync

body.raw:
{
  "plans": [
    {
      "uuid": "",
      "route_id": 1,
      "name": "Postman Test Plan",
      "is_imported": false,
      "stages": [
        {
          "stage_number": 1,
          "route_id": 1,
          "date": "2026-05-01T00:00:00Z",
          "start_city_id": 564,
          "end_city_id": 328,
          "start_albergue_id": null,
          "end_albergue_id": null,
          "custom_start_notes": null,
          "custom_end_notes": null,
          "stage_notes": "First day - SJPP to Honto",
          "created_at": "2026-02-12T10:00:00Z",
          "updated_at": "2026-02-12T10:00:00Z"
        },
        {
          "stage_number": 2,
          "route_id": 1,
          "date": "2026-05-02T00:00:00Z",
          "start_city_id": 328,
          "end_city_id": 329,
          "start_albergue_id": null,
          "end_albergue_id": null,
          "custom_start_notes": null,
          "custom_end_notes": null,
          "stage_notes": "Day 2 - over the Pyrenees",
          "created_at": "2026-02-12T10:00:00Z",
          "updated_at": "2026-02-12T10:00:00Z"
        }
      ],
      "updated_at": "2026-02-12T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Creates a new plan by sending an empty UUID. Server should assign a UUID and return it.
Updates an existing plan from the same device with a newer timestamp. Server should accept the update.

exec: pm.test('Server keeps newer version (name not overwritten)', function () {

request.description: Sends an update with an older timestamp from the same device. Server should ignore it and keep its newer version.

exec: console.log('No conflict detected - stages might be identical or server updated in place');

request.description: Sends an update from a DIFFERENT device with DIFFERENT stages. Should trigger conflict detection — server keeps original and creates a conflict copy with plan_uuid referencing the original.

exec: pm.test('No new conflict plan created', function () {
// Count how many plans reference plan_uuid
console.log('Total plans after no-conflict sync:', before);

request.description: Sends an update from a DIFFERENT device but with IDENTICAL stages. Should NOT create a conflict — stages are the same.

exec: pm.test('Deleted plan not in active plans or has deleted_at', function () {
console.log('Plan removed from response (server filtered it)');

request.description: Soft-deletes a plan by sending it with a deleted_at timestamp. Server should mark it as deleted.

exec: console.log('Server has', json.plans.length, 'plans for this user');

request.description: Sends an empty plans array. Server should return ALL plans for the authenticated user. Useful for initial sync on a new device.
Sends multiple new plans in a single sync request. Verifies the server can handle batch creation.
Sends a request without the X-Device-ID header. Should return 400.
Sends a request without authentication. Should return 401.
